test.command: bun test server/app.test.ts server/errors.test.ts src/utils/errors.test.ts && bun run test:frontend
test.note: Full bun test has React Native typeof import parsing issue, but Jest frontend and backend error tests pass correctly

lint.note: 10 warnings for explicit any in test files, no errors

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add error message format convention: Prefer 'Unable to [action]. Please try again.' over 'Failed to...' for user-facing error messages. The frontend-error-integration feature shows inconsistency between services.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: src/services/users.ts uses user-friendly format, but src/services/listings.ts and src/services/claims.ts use 'Failed to...' in some functions (confirmPickup, markNoShow, submitRating, createListing, etc.)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document Expo Router error boundary placement: Wrap error boundaries at child layout level (app/(auth)/_layout.tsx, app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx) rather than root layout. Stack.Screen doesn't directly render children - it renders the child layout file.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: navigation-error-boundaries feature correctly implemented this pattern but it wasn't explicitly documented in AGENTS.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about session middleware error handling: Consider updating server/session.ts to throw UnauthorizedError instead of returning JSON directly for consistency with the typed error pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: server/session.ts:16-18 returns c.json({ message: 'Unauthorized' }, 401) directly instead of throw new UnauthorizedError(). This is inconsistent with claims/listings routes that throw typed errors.

rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented - jest.config.js is the source of truth for Jest configuration
already-documented - testing.md already covers Jest setup patterns

rejectedObservations.observation: HttpError base class pattern with statusCode
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented - architecture.md mentions typed errors, implementation is self-documenting

rejectedObservations.observation: Type assertion pattern for Hono status codes (as 400 | 404 | 409)
rejectedObservations.reason: implementation-detail - This is a TypeScript/Hono type system workaround, not a pattern that needs documentation

rejectedObservations.observation: testID prop convention for interactive components
rejectedObservations.reason: minor - Single instance in Button component, not systemic enough to document as convention

rejectedObservations.observation: Test isolation using helper function instead of actual createApp()
rejectedObservations.reason: minor - Acceptable testing pattern, not a documentation need

nonBlockingIssues.description: Screen-level isolation within tabs not implemented. Errors in one tab screen make all tabs inaccessible. VAL-ERR-024 expects 'other tabs remain functional' but this would require screen-level boundaries.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Consider adding screen-level ErrorBoundary wrappers within each tab screen for finer-grained isolation

nonBlockingIssues.description: confirm-pickup and rating endpoints return 404 directly instead of throwing NotFoundError. Pattern inconsistency with no-show endpoint.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Non-critical - endpoints don't have try-catch blocks so direct return is functionally correct

nonBlockingIssues.description: VAL-ERR-027 (Zod validation errors) and VAL-ERR-028 (session middleware) are scoped to error-handler-middleware but describe behaviors needed in other middleware
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: These assertions may need re-scoping to validation middleware and session middleware features respectively

nonBlockingIssues.description: Some service functions (listings.ts, claims.ts) use 'Failed to...' instead of user-friendly 'Unable to... Please try again.' format
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Update error fallback messages in src/services/listings.ts and src/services/claims.ts for consistency
